fkcompute.output — Symbolic Formatting¶

The output subpackage converts the raw numeric result dictionary into human-readable symbolic polynomial expressions using SymPy.

Note

All functions in this module require SymPy. Install it with pip install "fkcompute[symbolic]". The module-level constant SYMPY_AVAILABLE indicates whether SymPy is installed.

fkcompute.output.symbolic¶

Symbolic output functionality for FK computation results.

This module provides functions to convert FK computation results from their numerical coefficient format into human-readable symbolic polynomials using SymPy.

fkcompute.output.symbolic.check_sympy_available() → None[source]¶

Check if SymPy is available, raise ImportError if not.

fkcompute.output.symbolic.list_to_q_polynomial(q_polyL: List[List[int | str]]) → Expr[source]¶

Convert FK coefficient data to SymPy polynomial expression in quantum parameter q.

Parameters:

q_polyL – List of [power, coefficient] pairs representing a polynomial in q.

Returns:

Polynomial in the quantum parameter q.

Return type:

sympy.Expr

fkcompute.output.symbolic.matrix_to_polynomial(fk_result: Dict) → Expr[source]¶

Convert FK computation result to a complete symbolic polynomial expression.

Parameters:

fk_result – FK computation result dictionary.

Returns:

Complete FK polynomial in topological variables and q.

Return type:

sympy.Expr

fkcompute.output.symbolic.collect_by_variables(polynomial: Expr) → Expr[source]¶

Reorganize polynomial by collecting terms with common powers of all topological variables (everything except q).

Parameters:

polynomial – SymPy polynomial expression.

Returns:

Polynomial with terms collected by all non-q variables.

Return type:

sympy.Expr

fkcompute.output.symbolic.collect_by_x_powers(polynomial: Expr) → Expr¶

Reorganize polynomial by collecting terms with common powers of all topological variables (everything except q).

Parameters:

polynomial – SymPy polynomial expression.

Returns:

Polynomial with terms collected by all non-q variables.

Return type:

sympy.Expr

fkcompute.output.symbolic.format_symbolic_output(fk_result: Dict[str, Any], format_type: str = 'pretty', max_degree: int = None) → str[source]¶

Format FK computation result as human-readable symbolic expression.

Parameters:

  • fk_result – FK computation result dictionary.

  • format_type – Output format - “pretty”, “latex”, “inline”, “mathematica”, or “str”.

  • max_degree – Maximum degree to display (if None, shows all terms).

Returns:

Formatted string representation of the FK polynomial.

Return type:

str

fkcompute.output.symbolic.print_symbolic_result(fk_result: Dict[str, Any], format_type: str = 'pretty', max_degree: int = None, show_matrix: bool = False) → None[source]¶

Print FK computation result in symbolic form.

Parameters:

  • fk_result – FK computation result dictionary.

  • format_type – Output format - “pretty”, “latex”, “inline”, “mathematica”, or “str”.

  • max_degree – Maximum degree to display.

  • show_matrix – If True, also display the coefficient matrix.

Usage Examples¶

High-level formatting:

from fkcompute import fk
from fkcompute.output.symbolic import format_symbolic_output

result = fk([1, 1, 1], 2)

print(format_symbolic_output(result, "pretty"))
print(format_symbolic_output(result, "latex"))
print(format_symbolic_output(result, "mathematica"))

Full SymPy expression:

from fkcompute.output.symbolic import matrix_to_polynomial
import sympy as sp

poly = matrix_to_polynomial(result)
print(type(poly))        # <class 'sympy.core.add.Add'>
print(poly.free_symbols) # {x, q}

# Evaluate at x=1
x = sp.Symbol("x")
print(poly.subs(x, 1))

Q-polynomial from coefficient list:

from fkcompute.output.symbolic import list_to_q_polynomial

poly_q = list_to_q_polynomial([[0, -1], [2, 1]])
print(poly_q)   # q**2 - 1

Format Reference¶

format_type

Description

"pretty"

Multi-line Unicode (uses sympy.pretty).

"latex"

LaTeX string (uses sympy.latex).

"mathematica"

Wolfram Language syntax (uses sympy.printing.mathematica).

"inline"

One-line Python string (uses str).

"str"

Alias for "inline".
